questions.question: Which country recently announced its decision to withdraw from Open Skies treaty?
questions.explanation: The United States recently announced that the country will withdraw from the major global treaty named Open Skies treaty. The 35-nation treaty allows unarmed surveillance flights over the territories of member countries. US administration also highlighted that Russia has repeatedly violated the terms of the pact. It is expected that the US will pull out formally in six months, based on the withdrawal terms of the treaty. NATO allies and other countries have asked the US to remain in the treaty.

questions.question: Dibru-Saikhowa National Park, that was seen in news recently, is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Oil India Limited (OIL) has recently announced that it has received environmental clearance from the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC), for the extension of drilling and testing of hydrocarbons under the Dibru-Saikhowa National Park of Assam. While several environmental activists raised fears about the impact of the project in an ecologically sensitive park and biosphere reserve, OIL negated the claims. The drilling and testing of hydrocarbons are to be done at seven locations under the park.

questions.question: ‘Micro-comb’, that was in news recently, is associated with which field?
questions.explanation: A team of Australian scientists have recorded the world’s fastest internet data speed of 44.2 Terabits per second (Tbps) from a single light source. This speed is enough to download 1000 HD movies in a split second. The researchers used their new device with a single piece of equipment known as a micro-comb, that is made up of hundreds of high-quality invisible infrared lasers from a single optical chip. This result can expand the capacity of network connections across the world, especially the developing and under-developed countries.

questions.question: Jitendra Nath Pande, who recently passed away, was associated with which field?
questions.explanation: Veteran Physician and former head of the Department of Medicine at the All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS) Dr Jitendra Nath Pande recently passed away at the age of 79. He and his wife had earlier contracted corona virus and remained in home isolation. He has contributed a lot in the field of pulmonology. He studied in the AIIMS and retired as the HOD of Medicine in the same institute. Even after retirement, he continued to mentor the practicing doctors and surgeons at AIIMS.

questions.question: Which Indian Army officer is selected for the prestigious United Nations Military Gender Advocate of the Year Award (2019)?
questions.explanation: An Indian Army officer, Major Suman Gawani, has been selected for the prestigious United Nations Military Gender Advocate of the Year Award (2019). This is the first time in history, that an Indian is awarded the mentioned award, that was created in 2016. The officer has served with the United Nations Mission in South Sudan (UNMISS). She has been selected for her outstanding contribution towards peacekeeping efforts in the UNMISS. Indian major will receive the award during an online ceremony, along with Brazilian Naval Officer Commander Carla Monteiro de Castro Araujo.

questions.question: AR500C, that was seen in news recently, is the first unmanned helicopter drone of which country?
questions.explanation: The first unmanned helicopter drone of China, named AR500C was seen in news as the helicopter made its first successful flight recently. It has been designed to fly in plateau areas and capable of firing and conducting investigation from a high altitude. The unmanned helicopter is expected to be deployed along the country’s borders with India, to safe-guard the southwestern borders in Tibet.

questions.question: Which organisation has launched an artificial intelligence (AI) based chatbot named ‘PAi’?
questions.explanation: National Payment Corporation of India (NPCI), that operates retail payments and settlement systems in India has recently launched an artificial intelligence (AI) based chatbot named ‘PAi’. The virtual assistant has been launched with an aim to create awareness about its flagship products like FASTag, RuPay, UPI, AePS on a real time basis. Users can send queries about the products and they get verified automated responses. Global RuPay Cardholders can access the chatbot.

questions.question: When is the ‘World Vape Day’ proposed to be celebrated across the world?
questions.explanation: About 40 million e-cigarette users from across the globe are set to celebrate the World Vape Day on May 30. It is celebrated to spread awareness on vaping and its effectiveness in harm reduction and helping smokers quit. The representative body of e-cigarette users in India the Association of Vapers India (AVI). It joins hands with consumer advocacy organisations from 34 nations to spread awareness. Last year, the Central Government imposed a ban against vaping.

questions.question: Which state has launched the ‘Rozgar Setu’ scheme for skilled migrant workers?
questions.explanation: The state of Madhya Pradesh has recently launched a new scheme called ‘Rozgar Setu’ scheme, with an aim of providing employment to the skilled workers who have returned to the state. The state is surveying the skilled workers who were employed at industries in other states and have returned now. The government will contact the owners of industries and factories and contractors associated with infrastructure projects such as road and bridge construction.

questions.question: Which private airline of India has been approved by the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) to conduct drone trials?
questions.explanation: India’s leading private air carrier SpiceJet announced that it has received formal approval from the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) to conduct drone trials. The dedicated cargo arm of SpiceJet, SpiceXpress was granted permission for conducting experimental Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) operations. SpiceXpress is planning to use drones to deliver medical, essential supplies and e-commerce products to the remote places of the country.
